front: Poet: 'my poems are naughty, but my life is pure' (vita proba est)
Poet: Tom Brown evaded expulsion by translating one of his poems as 'I do not love thee, Dr. Fell'
Poet: accuses Tongilianus of arson for insurance
Poet: doctor Symmachus gives him a cold with 'frigid hands'
Poet: wrote about gladiators Priscus and Verus
Poet: in retirement gloated that Juvenal might be 'wandering in the noisy Suburra'
Poet: wrote 'Let all girls read Sulpicia if they want to please their husband alone'
Poet: the word 'plagiarism' etymologically derives from his poem insulting Fidentius — before him, the Latin word meant 'to kidnap'
Poet: lapdog Issa described as 'naughtier than Catullus's sparrow'
Poet: earliest work describes the opening of the Colosseum — fights between humans and polar bears from Caledonia
Poet: wrote Xenia and Apophoreta — two sets of poems accompanying Saturnalia gifts
